findings.message: The client now sends params={"include_location": "true"} on GET /inventory/{item_id}, but the consumer Pact test (tests/functional/test_consumer_inventory.py) declares with_request("GET", "/inventory/1") with NO query parameters. The committed consumer contract and the real client request have diverged: the Pact mock server is set up to match a request with no query string, so either the consumer contract test now fails on a request mismatch, or the pact must be regenerated to include include_location=true (an added request expectation the provider verification must now satisfy). The pact's with_request was not updated to reflect the new query parameter.
findings.suggestion: Update the consumer pact in tests/functional/test_consumer_inventory.py to express the new request shape (e.g. .with_request("GET", "/inventory/1").with_query_parameter("include_location", "true")) and regenerate/publish the pact so the contract matches the client, and so the provider is verified against the include_location query. Confirm the provider actually honors include_location before adding it as a contracted expectation.
